grantTitle: COP-PILOT Open Call #1 - Edge Intelligence for Grid Reliability
programName: COP-PILOT (Collaborative Open Platform for PILOTing services across emerging smart IoT and Edge computing environments)
summary: COP-PILOT Open Call #1 offers up to EUR 200,000 per project (100% funding) for SME-led initiatives focused on edge intelligence for enhancing grid reliability in renewable energy-rich distribution grids in Preveza, Greece. The program expects to fund 8 projects across clusters, with 1-2 projects specifically for the CL3e cluster focusing on smart grid and renewable energy management using the COP-PILOT platform.
objectives: Deploy innovative edge intelligence solutions for grid reliability in renewable energy-rich distribution grids
Validate and provide feedback on COP-PILOT platform components through real-world implementations
Demonstrate cross-domain collaboration and interoperability using standardized APIs and protocols
Advance technology readiness levels from TRL 5-6 to TRL 7-8 through pilot implementations
Expand the COP-PILOT ecosystem with tangible innovative assets in smart grid and renewable energy domains
Ensure adherence to open standards (TMForum, ETSI ZSM, NGSI-LD, FIWARE, Eclipse Arrowhead)

requirements.description: Organization must be registered in an EU Member State or Horizon Europe Associated Country
requirements.evidence: Legal registration documents from eligible country

requirements.description: Leading partner MUST be an SME. Consortia must have at least 2 partners (SME-led)
requirements.evidence: SME declaration, consortium agreement if applicable

requirements.description: Not bankrupt, subject to insolvency proceedings, or guilty of grave professional misconduct or fraud
Compliant with tax and social security obligations
requirements.evidence: Tax compliance certificates, social security payment records

requirements.description: Proposal must be submitted in English only
Proposal must not exceed 12 pages
Complete proposal submission by deadline: 04 May 2026, 22:00 CET
Maximum funding request of EUR 200,000 per project
Indirect costs maximum 25% of direct costs
requirements.evidence: Budget table with indirect costs calculation

requirements.description: Subcontracting maximum 20% of total budget with clear justification
Travel costs limited to max EUR 1,000 per person per month
Cannot receive funding from another EU source for the same work
Application must be containerized and expose performance metrics
requirements.evidence: Container images, deployment manifests, metrics specification

requirements.description: Compliance with TMForum APIs for service/resource orchestration
Compliance with ETSI ZSM for zero-touch network management
Use NGSI-LD/NGSI-v2 for data management
Integration with FIWARE and/or Eclipse Arrowhead for IoT
Support integration protocols: MQTT, OPC UA, REST
Use COP-PILOT platform components (ServOrch, SIF, LLM-UI)
Compliance with GAIA-X framework
Publish application to git-based repository (GitHub/GitLab)
TRL progression typically from TRL 5-6 to TRL 7-8
Mid-term report submission at Month 4
Final report submission at Month 8 (with 2 months for reporting = Month 9)
GDPR compliance throughout project execution
requirements.evidence: Data protection impact assessment, GDPR compliance plan

requirements.description: Open access: publish findings and share code on GitHub/GitLab
requirements.evidence: Open access publication plan, public repository

requirements.description: Acknowledge EU funding with Grant Agreement: 101189819
requirements.evidence: EU funding acknowledgment in all materials

requirements.description: Maintain financial records for 6 years after project end

evaluationCriteria.name: Excellence
evaluationCriteria.description: Assessment of objectives, problem statement, technical approach, standards compliance, and innovation level
keyIndicators: SMART objectives with clear problem statement
Strong alignment with COP-PILOT goals and CL3e cluster objectives (grid reliability, renewable integration, edge intelligence)
Sound technical approach using FIWARE, TMForum APIs, Eclipse Arrowhead, GAIA-X compliance
Standards compliance (TMForum, ETSI ZSM, NGSI-LD, FIWARE)
Integration protocol implementation (MQTT, OPC UA, REST)
State-of-the-art analysis demonstrating novel approach
Clear TRL progression from TRL 5-6 to TRL 7-8
Minimum score of 3 (Good) required

evaluationCriteria.name: Impact
evaluationCriteria.description: Assessment of cluster targeting, platform integration, cross-sector support, and sustainability planning
keyIndicators: Specific targeting of CL3e cluster and smart grid use cases
Clear description of COP-PILOT platform integration approach
Effective leveraging of platform components (ServOrch, SIF, LLM-UI)
Support for cross-sector scenarios and applications
Comprehensive sustainability and dissemination plans
Strong open access and data sharing commitments
Minimum score of 3 (Good) required

evaluationCriteria.name: Implementation
evaluationCriteria.description: Assessment of project planning, team capabilities, timeline realism, and budget justification
keyIndicators: Clear project phases: Design, Implementation, Testing, Evaluation/Reporting
Mandatory milestones included: M4 intermediate report, M9 final deliverable
Realistic 8-month timeline with detailed Gantt chart
Strong team qualifications and relevant track record
Budget ≤ EUR 200,000 with detailed, justified breakdown
Indirect costs ≤ 25% of direct costs
Minimum score of 3 (Good) required

budget.totalAmount: EUR 1,600,000 total program budget (EUR 200,000 max per project)

categories.description: Salaries and wages for project staff working on the project
rules: Must be directly related to project activities
Maximum EUR 1,000 per person per month
Must be justified and necessary for project
Depreciation costs only (not full purchase price)
Must be directly used for project activities
Must comply with best value for money principle
Must be directly attributable to project

targetBeneficiaries: Research institutions and universities (as consortium partners)
Organizations working on smart grid technologies
Companies focused on renewable energy management
Edge computing and IoT solution providers
Grid reliability and energy distribution specialists
geographicScope: Pilot location: Preveza, Greece (CL3e cluster). Applicants must be from EU Member States or Horizon Europe Associated Countries.
eligibleCountries: Horizon Europe Associated Countries (including Norway, Iceland, Israel, Switzerland, United Kingdom, and others as per Horizon Europe association agreements)
additionalNotes: This is specifically for CL3e cluster (Edge Intelligence for Grid Reliability) located in Preveza, Greece. Other clusters exist but are not detailed in this call.
Program expects to fund 8 projects total across all clusters, with 1-2 projects per cluster
Overall threshold for passing: Individual score ≥3 (Good) in each criterion AND total score ≥60 points
Late submissions will be rejected - strict deadline enforcement
Payment structure: 50% upfront upon contract signature, 50% upon final report approval
Two primary workflows available: (1) Onboarding New Services for Service Providers, (2) Integrating New Infrastructure Domains for Infrastructure Owners
Key platform technologies include: Next.js, React, Keycloak, Apache Quarkus, Spring Boot, Kubernetes, OpenShift, OpenZiti, Prometheus, Grafana
Must use compatible infrastructure controllers such as Kubernetes
CI pipeline will auto-translate applications to standardized service blueprints
Domain Orchestrator (DO) and Data Management (DM) components must be run for infrastructure integration
Security requirements include CNCF SPIFFE, OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, X.509/PKI, mTLS
Projects must demonstrate cross-domain collaboration with existing COP-PILOT piloting domains
Financial records must be maintained for 6 years after project completion for audit purposes
Open access requirements include publishing findings and sharing code on public repositories (GitHub/GitLab)
Applications focusing on smart grid, renewable energy management, and edge computing for real-time monitoring have highest relevance for CL3e cluster
